userNote: Line 21 promises 'Locked files get a red lock indicator above the building.' but no code in dive-in.js (or anywhere in src/) actually renders such an indicator. wave8aOutcome: Comment-only fix per the NO CHEATS rule (option (a); sprite implementation is L4 and out-of-scope for 8A). The dive-in.js header already carried a 'NOT YET IMPLEMENTED' marker + a deferral note referring identity-and-analysis ticket 12 / Wave 3C. userNote: The Louis design memo is fragmented across the bible. Batch 021 (lines 2527-2610) holds the canonical Path C plan + the 50-line chmod primitive. References to Louis surface in batch 023 (hooks-architecture: 'future modules — Louis, plugins...'), in the LifecyclePhase/FileStatus enum tables (LOCKED slot reserved), and in the constellation's LOCKED color slot. A new contributor cannot read 'the Louis spec' as one document. docs/components/louis-and-locking.md consolidates everything in one place — this ticket exists to track the fact that the bible itself is not the canonical implementation spec; the docs/components/ entry is. If/when batches 028+ touch Louis again, the bible entry should link forward to the consolidated doc, not duplicate it.

Out-of-scope-for-st8 follow-up. The founder mentioned 'a couple errors but I've built it successfully before' about the standalone PyQt6 app. Louis/FIXES_APPLIED.md documents v1→v2 fixes (setup-wizard timing, path handling, layout-clearing on None, folder-checkboxes init order) but PyQt6 widget lifecycle bugs are notoriously easy to miss. Right places to audit if/when the standalone app gets attention again: LouisTab.__init__, ConnieTab.__init__, CarlTab.__init__ — race conditions on signal connect and uninitialized widget refs. Does NOT block the st8 port; Path C reimplements the Warden in Node, so any residual Python bugs do not migrate. Note: the Louis/ directory is not present in this working tree (uploaded by founder at commit 1df677b on master); this ticket is a future-self reminder, not an immediately actionable item.
wave8aOutcome: Boundary documented in docs/components/louis-and-locking.md under a new 'Louis-standalone tool boundary (Wave 8A explicit decision)' subsection inside 'Out of scope'. The doc now explicitly states: (1) Python bugs in lock_em_up_louis_v2.py are tracked outside this repo, (2) the file is reference-only — treat like OGB/, never imported, never edited from inside st8 sprint work, (3) lifecycle bugs found while reading get raised upstream / with the founder, not filed as st8 tickets, (4) Connie + Carl remain off-limits even if Louis-standalone is later deprecated. Closes the 'where do Python bugs go?' ambiguity that this ticket flagged.

userNote: Document the boundary explicitly so future contributors do not over-scope the port. The founder chose Path C (Warden only) and explicitly excluded Connie (database-to-LLM converter) and Carl (LLM chat context generator) from st8 integration. Without this written down, a well-meaning agent reading lock_em_up_louis_v2.py top-to-bottom might 'helpfully' port all 1463 lines. The 'Out of scope' section of louis-and-locking.md states this — verify it stays in place and survives future doc rewrites.

Forward-looking schema change. file_registry has 22 columns today; Phase L1 of the louis-and-locking roadmap needs one more — `locked INTEGER DEFAULT 0` — plus an idx_file_registry_locked index for the panel's listLocked() query. Decide D1 in louis-and-locking.md (column vs separate table) before adding. Default recommendation: add the column, single boolean is all the MVP carries. The LOCKED enum value already exists in the FileStatus and LifecyclePhase tables (lines 57, 60) but is never written, so adding a write-path is gated on this column existing.

Forward-looking — register HOOKS.LOCK_CHANGED in the canonical map. Payload: { fingerprint, filepath, locked }. Two default subscribers (per louis-and-locking.md spec): P=10 regenerate-protected-file (writes ~/.louis-control/protected-files.txt from SQLite), P=20 sse-broadcast-lock-change (pushes { type: 'lock-change', fingerprint, locked } to /api/mutations clients). Matches the existing pattern used for COMMIT_RECORDED (batch 024) and TICKET_CREATED (batch 026 area).

Forward-looking — three new routes. POST /api/lock { path } → resolves filepath via file_registry, calls features/locks/lock-manager.lockFile(absPath), persists via features/locks/lock-state.setLocked(fingerprint, true), fires HOOKS.LOCK_CHANGED. POST /api/unlock symmetric. GET /api/locks → returns features/locks/lock-state.listLocked(). All three share the same path-resolution helper. Mount the routes next to /api/record-commit and /api/tickets which follow the same hook-fire-after-write pattern.

Forward-looking — extend the existing symlink installer to manage a pre-commit hook in addition to the post-commit hook batch 024 installed. The pre-commit script reads ~/.louis-control/protected-files.txt and rejects commits to listed paths (see louis-and-locking.md for the shell-script body). install.sh becomes the single entry point for both git hooks; the lock panel UI shows their install status side by side. Idempotent on re-install; refuses to overwrite a user-customized pre-commit (panel surfaces the conflict).

No code change needed — STATUS_COLOR.LOCKED already reserves the --pink token ({r:201, g:116, b:143}). This ticket exists only to flag: when Phase L2 wires the SSE 'lock-change' event into the constellation, the implementation must call window.St8Constellation.updateFileStatus(fingerprint, 'LOCKED'), and statusColor(file) must check file.locked before file.status (per frontend-experience.md:326 'Locked overrides status'). Verify both behaviors hold once the lock SSE wiring lands.
wave8aOutcome: Annotated. Confirmed STATUS_COLOR.LOCKED is fully wired post-Wave-7C: src/frontend/components/status-colors.js is the single-source module exposing INT/HEX/RGB tables (LOCKED: 0xC9748F = --pink, 'louis-protected'). Both constellation.js and dive-in.js read from window.St8StatusColors with inline fallbacks. statusColor(file) at status-colors.js:89,95 already returns LOCKED.INT/RGB when file.locked is truthy — locked overrides status. The data side (file.locked truthy) is the only thing missing; that's Phase L1. Cross-link added to louis-and-locking.md touchpoint table.

userNote: Forward-looking — add a lock badge next to each row's status dot. Renders a small 🔒 glyph in the LOCKED-pink token when file.locked === 1. Subscribes to the same SSE 'lock-change' event as the constellation; single subscription, two render sinks. Likely sits in the existing render loop with one new conditional CSS class. Depends on the file-explorer already exposing per-row data binding; if it currently re-renders the whole tree on every update, that may need refactoring first (separate concern, file in frontend-experience cluster).

Forward-looking — file does not exist yet. Phase L1 of the louis-and-locking roadmap creates it. Exports lockFile / unlockFile / isWritable / lockAll / unlockAll. ~50 LOC. Pure fs.chmodSync wrapper. No SQLite, no SSE — stateless primitive. Body is given verbatim in louis-and-locking.md §'The chmod primitive'. Windows caveat documented there: fs.chmodSync only flips the read-only bit; lock is a courtesy not a security mechanism.

Forward-looking — file does not exist yet. Phase L1 of the louis-and-locking roadmap creates it. Owns SQLite reads/writes for the lock bit (file_registry.locked column per D1 default). Exports setLocked / getLocked / listLocked / regenerateProtectedFile. ~100 LOC. Rides existing st8.sqlite; no separate DB. regenerateProtectedFile() writes ~/.louis-control/protected-files.txt from `SELECT filepath FROM file_registry WHERE locked = 1`; called by the P=10 default subscriber on HOOKS.LOCK_CHANGED.

Forward-looking — file does not exist yet. Phase L3 of the louis-and-locking roadmap creates it. Ports Louis's install_git_hook() into Node. Writes .git/hooks/pre-commit with a versioned header, checks for prior customization, idempotent on re-install. Co-managed with the existing post-commit hook (batch 024) via scripts/git-hooks/install.sh. ~80 LOC.

Forward-looking — file does not exist yet. Phase L2 of the louis-and-locking roadmap creates it. Renders a toggle list of every file_registry row with lock state; search box; 'Lock all PRODUCTION' bulk button (gated by D4 policy decision). Mount target: the Settings panel slot (or a new outer-edge dock diamond per the batch-027 design memo). Subscribes to SSE 'lock-change' for live updates. ~200 LOC. Must NOT carry inline styles — pending-tickets/frontend-experience.json has multiple high-severity tickets about dive-in.js's inline-style soup; do not repeat that mistake.

Forward-looking — bootstrap migration question. When the lock column first lands, what about files already chmod 444'd on disk (pre-existing read-only files, files Louis-standalone locked)? Bootstrap should SELECT filepath FROM file_registry, isWritable()-check each, and seed locked = 1 for paths the OS reports read-only. Without this step the first-run state is inconsistent — SQLite says unlocked, OS says locked, panel UI shows lies. Bundle with the column-add migration in Phase L1.

Forward-looking — open question on lock-history audit trail. Louis writes ~/.louis-control/lock-history.log line-by-line. st8 already has file_mutation_log (with mutationType enum that INCLUDES 'LOCK' but never fires it — see bible line 1617's 'Defined-but-never-fired' note). Right merge: a LOCK_CHANGED default subscriber that INSERTs a row into file_mutation_log with mutationType='LOCK', actor=(future) 'DEVELOPER'|'AGENT'|'SYSTEM'. Removes the need for a separate lock-history.log inside ~/.louis-control. Decide before Phase L1 lands so the hook-firing path is right the first time.
wave8aOutcome: DECIDED: option (a) — st8 reuses existing audit surfaces; no separate lock-history.log file. Recorded in docs/components/louis-and-locking.md 'Known unknowns' section (the bullet titled 'Lock-history audit trail — DECIDED (Wave 8A, ticket 15)'). The decision binds two existing tables: (1) file_mutation_log gets a LOCK row per change via the P=20 lock-mutation-log subscriber (finally firing the long-declared LOCK enum value), (2) activity_log gets a parallel row via a P=30 lock-activity-log subscriber for the cross-cutting timeline. Both tables exist today at persistence.js:117 (file_mutation_log) and persistence.js:129 (activity_log) with the right schema (targetFingerprint, action, source, details, timestamp). The Phase L1 hook-firing path is now spec'd, no further open question. No source edit needed in default-subscribers.js this wave — the subscribers themselves are L1 work; this ticket only had to PICK the audit-surface strategy, which is now done.
